001import java.util.HashMap;
002
003import java.io.File;
004import java.io.FileNotFoundException;
005import java.util.Scanner;
006
007/**
008 * GameEngine class - handles the game logic of the text adventure "Henriette's Feast".
009 * It processes commands, manages the current room and movement history,
010 * and interacts with the user interface.
011 *
012 * @author Michael Kolling and David J. Barnes + D. Bureau + Raphaël Quillaud
013 * @version 2026.05.09
014 */
015public class GameEngine {
016    private Player aPlayer;
017    private Parser aParser;
018    private HashMap<String, Room> aRooms;
019    private UserInterface aGui;
020
021    /**
022     * Constructor for GameEngine.
023     * Initializes the rooms, parser, and movement history.
024     */
025    public GameEngine() {
026        this.aRooms = new HashMap<String, Room>();
027        this.aParser = new Parser();
028        this.createRooms();
029    }
030
031    /**
032     * Creates all rooms of the village and connects them with exits.
033     * Also sets the starting room (Henriette's kitchen).
034     */
035    private void createRooms() {
036        // Rooms
037        Room vKitchen       = new Room("in Henriette's warm, cosy kitchen","Images/kitchen.jpg");
038        Room vLivingRoom    = new Room("in the living room, next to the Christmas tree","Images/livingRoom.jpg");
039        Room vGarden        = new Room("in the snowy front garden of the house","Images/garden.jpg");
040        Room vUnderTreeHouse = new Room("under the tree house, in the snow","Images/underTreeHouse.jpg");
041        Room vTreeHouse     = new Room("in the children's tree house","Images/treeHouse.jpg");
042        Room vFrontHouse    = new Room("in the street, right in front of Henriette's house", "Images/frontHouse.jpg");
043        Room vThereseHouse  = new Room("in Thérèse's house, the neighbour. Look ! She left a cookie on the table with a note that says : 'You'll thank me later ;)'", "Images/thereseHouse.jpg");
044        Room vHouseStreet   = new Room("in the quiet street in front of the houses", "Images/houseStreet.jpg");
045        Room vBakery        = new Room("in the bakery that smells of fresh bread", "Images/bakery.jpg");
046        Room vMainStreet    = new Room("on the main street decorated with garlands", "Images/mainStreet.jpg");
047        Room vButcherShop   = new Room("in the butcher's shop, where a savoury smell of roasts fills the air", "Images/butcherShop.jpg");
048        Room vMainSquare    = new Room("on the village square, under the huge sparkling Christmas tree", "Images/mainSquare.jpg");
049        Room vGrocery       = new Room("in the small village grocery store", "Images/grocery.jpg");
050
051        // Store Rooms in the map
052        this.aRooms.put("kitchen", vKitchen);
053        this.aRooms.put("livingRoom", vLivingRoom);
054        this.aRooms.put("garden", vGarden);
055        this.aRooms.put("underTreeHouse", vUnderTreeHouse);
056        this.aRooms.put("treeHouse", vTreeHouse);
057        this.aRooms.put("frontHouse", vFrontHouse);
058        this.aRooms.put("thereseHouse", vThereseHouse);
059        this.aRooms.put("houseStreet", vHouseStreet);
060        this.aRooms.put("bakery", vBakery);
061        this.aRooms.put("mainStreet", vMainStreet);
062        this.aRooms.put("butcherShop", vButcherShop);
063        this.aRooms.put("mainSquare", vMainSquare);
064        this.aRooms.put("grocery", vGrocery);
065
066        // Exits
067            // Kitchen
068        vKitchen.setExit("east", vLivingRoom);
069
070            // Living room
071        vLivingRoom.setExit("east", vGarden);
072        vLivingRoom.setExit("west", vKitchen);
073
074            // Garden
075        vGarden.setExit("north", vUnderTreeHouse);
076        vGarden.setExit("east", vFrontHouse);
077        vGarden.setExit("west", vLivingRoom);
078
079            // Under Treehouse
080        vUnderTreeHouse.setExit("south", vGarden);
081        vUnderTreeHouse.setExit("up", vTreeHouse);
082
083            // Treehouse
084        vTreeHouse.setExit("down", vUnderTreeHouse);
085
086            // Front of the house
087        vFrontHouse.setExit("south", vHouseStreet);
088        vFrontHouse.setExit("west", vGarden);
089
090            // Therese's house
091        vThereseHouse.setExit("north", vGarden);
092        vThereseHouse.setExit("east", vHouseStreet);
093
094            // House street
095        vHouseStreet.setExit("north", vFrontHouse);
096        vHouseStreet.setExit("south", vMainStreet);
097        vHouseStreet.setExit("west", vThereseHouse);
098
099            // Bakery
100        vBakery.setExit("east", vMainStreet);
101
102            // Main street
103        vMainStreet.setExit("north", vHouseStreet);
104        vMainStreet.setExit("east", vButcherShop);
105        vMainStreet.setExit("south", vMainSquare);
106        vMainStreet.setExit("west", vBakery);
107
108            // Butcher's shop
109        vButcherShop.setExit("west", vMainStreet);
110
111            // Main square
112        vMainSquare.setExit("north", vMainStreet);
113        vMainSquare.setExit("east", vGrocery);
114
115            // Grocery
116        vGrocery.setExit("west", vMainSquare);
117
118        // Items
119        Item vRecipeBook = new Item("book", "Henriette's Christmas recipe book", 2);
120        Item vFlour = new Item("flour","a bag of flour", 1);
121        Item vButter = new Item("butter","a block of butter", 1);
122        Item vSugar = new Item("sugar", "a packet of sugar", 1);
123        Item vChocolate = new Item("chocolate", "a bar of dark chocolate", 1);
124        Item vTurkey = new Item("turkey","a beautiful Christmas turkey", 5);
125        Item vSpices = new Item("spices","a small box of Christmas spices", 1);
126        Item vWoodLog = new Item("log","a dry log for the fireplace", 6);
127        Item vPlate = new Item("plate", "a large serving plate, perfect for the Christmas dinner", 3);
128        Item vCookie = new Item("cookie", "a delicious cookie made by love by Thérèse. Why is it so heavy though ? ...", 5);
129
130        // Place items in rooms
131        vKitchen.addItem(vRecipeBook);
132        vBakery.addItem(vFlour);
133        vGrocery.addItem(vButter);
134        vGrocery.addItem(vSpices);
135        vGrocery.addItem(vSugar);
136        vGrocery.addItem(vChocolate);
137        vButcherShop.addItem(vTurkey);
138        vUnderTreeHouse.addItem(vWoodLog);
139        vThereseHouse.addItem(vCookie);
140        vThereseHouse.addItem(vPlate);
141
142        this.aPlayer = new Player("Henriette", vKitchen, 5);
143    }
144
145    /**
146     * Tries to go to the room in the direction given by the command.
147     * If there is no second word or no exit in that direction,
148     * prints an error message.
149     *
150     * @param pCommand the command containing the direction
151     */
152    private void goRoom(final Command pCommand) {
153        if (!pCommand.hasSecondWord()) {
154            this.aGui.println("Go where ?");
155            return;
156        }
157
158        String vDirection = pCommand.getSecondWord();
159        Room vNextRoom = this.aPlayer.getCurrentRoom().getExit(vDirection);
160
161        if (vNextRoom == null) {
162            this.aGui.println("There is no door !");
163            return;
164        }
165
166        this.aPlayer.goTo(vNextRoom);
167        printLocationInfo();
168    }
169
170    /**
171     * Executes the 'look' command. Prints the current room description
172     * and the items present in the room.
173     */
174    private void look() {
175        this.aGui.println(this.aPlayer.getCurrentRoom().getLongDescription());
176    }
177
178    /**
179     * Executes the 'eat' command. If the player eats a cookie, their maximum 
180     * weight capacity is doubled. Other items cannot be eaten.
181     *
182     * @param pCommand the command containing the name of the item to eat
183     */
184    private void eat(final Command pCommand) {
185        String vItemName = pCommand.getSecondWord().toLowerCase();
186
187        if (!this.aPlayer.hasItem(vItemName)) {
188            this.aGui.println("You are not carrying any '" + vItemName + "'.");
189            return;
190        }
191
192        if (vItemName.equals("cookie")) {
193            this.aPlayer.drop(vItemName);
194            this.aPlayer.setMaxWeight(this.aPlayer.getMaxWeight() * 2);
195            this.aGui.println("You eat the cookie that Thérèse left you. You ask yourself why it is so heavy...");
196            this.aGui.println("Your carrying capacity has doubled ! " + this.aPlayer.getWeightString());
197        }
198        else {
199            this.aGui.println("You can't eat that ! Keep everything for the dinner...");
200        }
201    }
202
203    /**
204     * Moves the player back to the previous room, if available.
205     * If there are no previous rooms in the navigation history, 
206     * a message is displayed indicating that the action is not possible.
207     * Otherwise, the last visited room is retrieved from the stack 
208     * and set as the current room, and the location information is updated.
209     */
210    private void back() {
211        if (!this.aPlayer.goBack()) {
212            this.aGui.println("You can't go back !");
213            return;
214        }
215
216        printLocationInfo();
217    }
218
219    /**
220     * Picks up an item from the current room and adds it to the player's inventory.
221     * @param pCommand the command containing the name of the item to take
222     */
223    private void take(final Command pCommand) {
224        if (!pCommand.hasSecondWord()) {
225            this.aGui.println("Take what ?");
226            return;
227        }
228
229        String vItemName = pCommand.getSecondWord().toLowerCase();
230        Item vItem = this.aPlayer.getCurrentRoom().getItem(vItemName);
231
232        if (vItem == null) {
233            this.aGui.println("There is no item called '" + vItemName + "' here.");
234            return;
235        }
236
237        if (!this.aPlayer.pickUp(vItem)) {
238            this.aGui.println("Too heavy ! " + this.aPlayer.getWeightString());
239            return;
240        }
241
242        this.aPlayer.getCurrentRoom().removeItem(vItemName);
243        this.aGui.println("You picked up : " + vItem.getName());
244    }
245    /**
246     * Drops the item currently carried by the player into the current room.
247     * @param pCommand the command containing the name of the item to drop
248     */
249    private void drop(final Command pCommand) {
250        if (!pCommand.hasSecondWord()) {
251            this.aGui.println("Drop what ? " + this.aPlayer.getInventoryString());
252            return;
253        }
254
255        String vItemName = pCommand.getSecondWord().toLowerCase();
256        Item vDropped = this.aPlayer.drop(vItemName);
257
258        if (vDropped == null) {
259            this.aGui.println("You are not carrying '" + vItemName + "'.");
260            return;
261        }
262
263        this.aPlayer.getCurrentRoom().addItem(vDropped);
264        this.aGui.println("You dropped : " + vDropped.getName());
265    }
266    /**
267     * Tests the commands provided in a specified file by interpreting and executing them sequentially.
268     * The file should contain one command per line. Commands are ignored if the file is not found,
269     * or if the command cannot be interpreted.
270     * If the command does not have a second word specifying the file name, a usage message is displayed.
271     * If the specified file cannot be located, an error message is displayed.
272     *
273     * @param pCommand the command containing the file name as the second word
274     */
275    private void test(final Command pCommand) {
276        if (!pCommand.hasSecondWord()) {
277            this.aGui.println("Test which file ? Usage : test <filename>");
278            return;
279        }
280
281        String vFileName = pCommand.getSecondWord() + ".txt";
282
283        try {
284            Scanner vScanner = new Scanner(new File(vFileName));
285            while (vScanner.hasNextLine()) {
286                String vLine = vScanner.nextLine().trim();
287                if (!vLine.isEmpty()) {
288                    this.interpretCommand(vLine);
289                }
290            }
291            vScanner.close();
292        } catch (FileNotFoundException e) {
293            this.aGui.println("Test file not found : " + vFileName);
294        }
295    }
296
297    /**
298     * Prints the welcome text and the initial location information.
299     */
300    private void printWelcome() {
301        this.aGui.println("""
302                
303                
304                *****************************
305                Welcome to "Henriette's Feast" !
306                It is Christmas Eve in a small, snowy village.
307                Grandma Henriette must prepare the big Christmas dinner
308                for the whole family, but she suddenly realizes she forgot
309                to do all the Christmas shopping.
310                
311                Explore the village, gather everything she needs
312                for the dinner, and come back to the kitchen in time !
313                
314                Type 'help' if you need help.
315                """);
316
317        printLocationInfo();
318    }
319
320    /**
321     * Prints the current room description and its available exits.
322     */
323    private void printLocationInfo() {
324        this.aGui.println("\n" + this.aPlayer.getCurrentRoom().getLongDescription() + "\n");
325
326        if (this.aPlayer.getCurrentRoom().getImageName() != null) {
327            this.aGui.showImage(this.aPlayer.getCurrentRoom().getImageName());
328        }
329    }
330
331    /**
332     * Prints a help message describing the game objective
333     * and the available commands.
334     */
335    private void printHelp() {
336        this.aGui.println("""
337                You are Grandma Henriette in a small snowy village.
338                You must find everything you need for the Christmas dinner.
339                
340                Your command words are:""");
341        this.aGui.println(this.aParser.getCommandList());
342    }
343
344    /**
345     * Interprets and executes the given command line.
346     *
347     * @param pCommandLine the command line entered by the user
348     */
349    public void interpretCommand(final String pCommandLine) {
350        this.aGui.println("> " + pCommandLine);
351        Command vCommand = this.aParser.getCommand(pCommandLine);
352
353        if (vCommand.isUnknown()) {
354            this.aGui.println("I don't know what you mean...");
355            return;
356        }
357
358        String vCommandWord = vCommand.getCommandWord();
359
360        switch (vCommandWord) {
361            case "go" :
362                this.goRoom(vCommand);
363                break;
364
365            case "help" :
366                this.printHelp();
367                break;
368
369            case "look" :
370                if (vCommand.hasSecondWord()) {
371                    this.aGui.println("You can't look at something in particular.");
372                }
373                else {
374                    this.look();
375                }
376                break;
377
378            case "eat" :
379                if (!vCommand.hasSecondWord()) {
380                    this.aGui.println("Eat what ?");
381                }
382                else {
383                    this.eat(vCommand);
384                }
385                break;
386
387            case "quit" :
388                if (vCommand.hasSecondWord()) {
389                    this.aGui.println("Quit what ?");
390                }
391                else {
392                    this.endGame();
393                }
394                break;
395
396            case "back" :
397                if (vCommand.hasSecondWord()) {
398                    this.aGui.println("Back what ?");
399                }
400                else {
401                    this.back();
402                }
403                break;
404
405            case "test" :
406                this.test(vCommand);
407                break;
408
409            case "take" :
410                this.take(vCommand);
411                break;
412
413            case "drop" :
414                this.drop(vCommand);
415                break;
416
417            case "items" :
418                this.aGui.println(this.aPlayer.getInventoryString());
419                this.aGui.println(this.aPlayer.getWeightString());
420                break;
421
422            default :
423                this.aGui.println("Programmer error: command not recognised!");
424                break;
425        }
426    }
427
428    /**
429     * Stores the graphical user interface and prints the welcome message.
430     *
431     * @param pUserInterface the graphical user interface of the game
432     */
433    public void setGUI(final UserInterface pUserInterface) {
434        this.aGui = pUserInterface;
435        this.printWelcome();
436    }
437
438    /**
439     * Ends the game by printing a goodbye message and disabling input.
440     */
441    private void endGame() {
442        this.aGui.println("Thank you for playing. Goodbye.");
443        this.aGui.enable(false);
444    }
445
446} // GameEngine
